ddls:
CREATE TABLE "participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the participant is part of */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the participant */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the participant */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the participant */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the participant */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the participant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_participants_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "studies" (
  "study_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each study */,
  "study_name" TEXT /* Name of the study */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the study */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the study */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the study */,
  "study_type" TEXT /* Type of the study (e.g., experiment, survey) */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher leading the study */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("study_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* ID of the participant who made the response */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the response belongs to */,
  "opt1" TEXT /* Option 1 chosen by the participant */,
  "opt2" TEXT /* Option 2 chosen by the participant */,
  "response" TEXT /* Response made by the participant */,
  "time" REAL /* Time taken to make the response */,
  "threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold value for the response */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date the response was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_participant_id FOREIGN KEY ("participant_id") REFERENCES participants ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_opt1 FOREIGN KEY ("opt1") REFERENCES options ("option_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_opt2 FOREIGN KEY ("opt2") REFERENCES options ("option_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "options" (
  "option_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each option */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the option belongs to */,
  "option_name" TEXT /* Name of the option */,
  "option_description" TEXT /* Description of the option */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("option_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "study_user" (
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the study (e.g., researcher, collaborator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("study_id", "user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "study_option" (
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study */,
  "option_id" INTEGER /* ID of the option */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Whether the option is required in the study */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("study_id", "option_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "response_values" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the response */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the threshold belongs to */,
  "threshold_value" INTEGER /* Value of the threshold */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of the threshold (e.g., time, score) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thresholds_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id")
)
column_names: ID of the study the participant is part of
Type of the study (e.g., experiment, survey)
ID of the researcher leading the study
ID of the participant who made the response
ID of the study the response belongs to
Time taken to make the response
ID of the study the option belongs to
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin)
Role of the user in the study (e.g., researcher, collaborator)
Whether the option is required in the study
ID of the study the threshold belongs to
Type of the threshold (e.g., time, score)
